Sam Heidari, Ph.D. 
 

 

 

 

 
CEO, Board Member
 

 

 

 

 
Sam Heidari brings over 30 years of leadership experience across a range of technology sectors. He is currently the CEO of Lumotive and serves on the board of Morse Micro. His previous roles include CEO and Chairman of Quantenna Communications, CEO of Doradus Technologies, CTO of Ikanos, and Venture Partner at Cota Capital.

Dr. Heidari has contributed significantly to the tech industry, serving on the Global Semiconductor Alliance Board of Directors and the advisory boards for the Electrical Engineering and Computer Science departments at the University of Southern California.

His achievements have earned him numerous recognitions, including 2014 Executive of the Year by the Golden Bridge Awards, finalist for EY Entrepreneur of the Year in 2016, and 2017 Executive of the Year by UBM. A senior member of IEEE, Dr. Heidari holds more than 50 U.S. and international patents.

He earned his M.S. and Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from the University of Southern California, and his B.S. in Electrical Engineering from Northeastern University.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
Gleb Akselrod, Ph.D. 
 

 

 

 

 
CTO, Founder, Board Member
 

 

 

 

 
Gleb Akselrod has more than 10 years experience in photonics and optoelectronics. Prior to Lumotive he was the Director for Optical Technologies at Intellectual Ventures in Bellevue, WA, where he led a program on the commercialization of optical metamaterial and nanophotonic technologies. 

Previously, he was a postdoctoral fellow in the Center for Metamaterials and Integrated Plasmonics at Duke University, where his work focused on plasmonic nanoantennas and metasurfaces. He completed his Ph.D. in 2013 at MIT, where he was a Hertz Foundation Fellow and an NSF Graduate Fellow studying excitons in materials for solar cells and OLEDs. Prior to MIT, Dr. Akselrod was at Landauer, Inc. where he developed and patented a fluorescent radiation sensor that is currently deployed by the US Army.

Dr. Akselrod received his Ph.D. in Physics from MIT and his B.S. in Engineering Physics from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign. He holds more than 30 US Patents and has published over 25 scientific articles.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
Apurva Jain, Ph.D. 
 

 

 

 

 
VP of Product and Customer Success
 

 

 

 

 
Apurva Jain is a recognized leader in optics, photonics, and semiconductor innovation, with over a decade of experience bringing transformative optical technologies to market. His expertise spans product strategy & innovation, manufacturing operations, and customer success, guiding products from concept through mass deployment.

Apurva has contributed to multiple patents and publications and has led cross-functional teams delivering breakthrough solutions in sensing, imaging, automation, networking, and communications. Prior to Lumotive, he held leadership roles at Avo Photonics, where he helped commercialize advanced optoelectronic products.
He earned a Ph.D. in Optics from CREOL, University of Central Florida, and a B.Eng. in Electrical Engineering from McGill University.

Beyond technology, Apurva is the founder of CCoffee (www.ccoffee.ca), a social impact venture that invests its profits into children’s education, reflecting his commitment to creating a positive global impact. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
Axel Fuchs, Ph.D.
 

 

 

 

 
VP of Business Development
 

 

 

 

 
Over the past three decades, Axel Fuchs has demonstrated a strong track record in managing key aspects of global high-tech businesses.

In 1995, Dr. Fuchs co-founded the Daimler Benz Research and Technology office. He later joined Motorola's newly established Telematics division, where he led the Systems Engineering Team. Since 2002, Dr. Fuchs has held leadership positions at several Silicon Valley startups, where he played a pivotal role in developing the automotive business and expanding the consumer segment for Simple Devices, which was acquired by Universal Electronics in 2004. He also led business development for Quanergy, growing the business from inception to $50 million.

Dr. Fuchs holds a Ph.D. in Control Engineering from Darmstadt University of Technology.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
Rakinder Grover
 

 

 

 

 
VP of Strategy and Partnerships
 

 

 

 

 
Rakinder Grover is a technology executive with over 25 years of experience. His expertise is in business management, business development and venture capital investing in high-growth, innovation-led companies. 

Prior to Lumotive, Mr. Grover held various management roles at Dolby Laboratories and Ikanos. As a Managing Director at Walden International, he was a venture capital investor in early-stage semiconductor and communications startups. 

Mr. Grover holds an MBA from The Wharton School of the University of Pennsylvania, a master’s degree in computer science from the University of Texas and a bachelor’s degree from the Indian Institute of Technology.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
Tristan Joo
 

 

 

 

 
EVP of Global Business
 

 

 

 

 
Tristan Joo brings 3 decades of extensive experience in tunable optics, imaging, and optical/3D sensors, successfully having scaled businesses, driven profitable revenue growth, and elevated companies to top industry positions. His specialty is in developing new business and partnership ecosystems with highly effective technical sales and marketing savvy at both small and global organizations. 

Prior to Lumotive, Tristan was VP of Business Development/Sales and Corporate Marketing at poLight ASA, an innovator in tunable optics as well as at Ofilm, a compact camera/3D sensing module integrator, both for compact cameras and microdisplays used in AR/MR, and consumer and industrial markets. He has also held executive business leadership roles at ams AG, Maxim Integrated, LensVector, and IBM Microelectronics, where he mass-marketed and sold many world's first or best in class Analog Mixed Signal, imaging/optical semiconductor solutions. 

Tristan holds an MBA from the Darden School at the University of Virginia and a Bachelor of Science degree in Electronics Engineering at Yonsei University in South Korea.
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
Diane Krzysik
 

 

 

 

 
VP of Human Resources
 

 

 

 

 
Diane Krzysik is a human resources executive with over 20 years of experience in all facets of human resources and business partnering. Her expertise is in Talent Acquisition, Talent Development and Nurturing a Vibrant and Inclusive Culture. She has deep experience in the following corporate life cycle changes: High-Growth, Public Transition Phase, Mergers & Acquisitions, Down Market Conditions, Restructuring, Management Changes and Market Transitions.
Prior to Lumotive, Ms. Krzysik held management roles at Atomwise and Quantenna. There she built out the HR function, scaled a global high growth start up, supported the company through an IPO and was part of the team that took them through an M&A and subsequent post acquisition transition to a Fortune 500 company. Prior to Quantenna, she held earlier director/manager positions at Arcwave, Carnegie Mellon University and TechTV.
Ms. Krzysik holds a Human Resource Management (HRM), Honors Distinction from the University of California Santa Cruz.
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
Conrad Burke 
 

 

 

 

 
Board Member
 

 

 

 

 
Conrad Burke is based in Silicon Valley and is managing partner at MetaVC Partners, LLC. He is an entrepreneur and investor, building successful new businesses in renewable energy, communications, nanomaterials, and biosciences. He was founder and CEO of energy materials startup, Innovalight (acquired by DuPont) and was venture partner at Sevin Rosen Funds and Invention Science Fund (ISF).

Some of his past and current board responsibilities include Capella Photonics (acquired by Alcatel-Lucent), Lumotive, Imagia, and the advisory boards of Aelurus (acquired by Broadcom), InnerAnalytics and Cendana Capital. For three years, Mr. Burke served on the board of SolarPower Europe - the European solar industry association. He was a Technology Pioneer at the World Economic Forum in Davos for renewable energy innovations and he is a recipient of the Ernst & Young Entrepreneur of the Year™ award. Conrad has extensive international experience having worked and lived in Japan, Germany, the U.K. at AT&T, NEC and Lucent Technologies.

He has a MS in Physics from Trinity College and a BS in Physics from University College Dublin in Ireland.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
Jim Anderson
 

 

 

 

 
Board Member
 

 

 

 

 
Jim Anderson was appointed Chief Executive Officer of Coherent Corp. and a member of the Board of Directors on June 3, 2024. He previously served as a director and as President and Chief Executive Officer of Lattice Semiconductor Corporation since September 2018.

To interact intelligently with the physical world, machines need more than just a camera feed. Voyant delivers the first scalable FMCW LiDAR with true on-chip beam steering. By replacing mechanical scanners with silicon photonics, we capture instant velocity and depth data without moving parts.

We provide a solid-state, software-configurable solution that is mass-producible and ready for the field.

 VIEW PRODUCTS 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

 
Digital Beam Steering, On-Chip. No Compromises.

 
 

 
From complex optics to a single monostatic module.

 
Voyant’s focal-plane array integrates FMCW LiDAR, beam steering, and coherent detection on a unified silicon photonics die — no moving parts, no bi-static alignment, no multi-chip assemblies. Our monostatic architecture delivers true solid-state reliability with software-defined scan control.

 

 

 
Manufactured like processors, not like optics.

 
Leveraging datacom-grade photonic foundries, our platform replaces bespoke LiDAR assemblies with wafer-scale fabrication. Thousands of optical components, digitally steered apertures, and coherent receivers are produced with semiconductor-level consistency, enabling performance and pricing that scale to millions of units.

 

 

 
Perception built for Physical AI.

 
FMCW sensing provides 4D data — depth and Velocity— with ambient-light immunity and long-range precision. This is the spatial foundation that AI systems need to operate autonomously in factories, warehouses, cities, and the open world. With transparent documentation and real-world results, we show exactly what our technology can do.

 

 
 

 

 
 
 

 
AI can't impact the real world if it can't see the real world

 
We’re not simply reducing the size and cost of LiDAR technology, we’re making it safer, more accurate, and more powerful than ever before.

 VIEW PRODUCTS 
 

 

 

 

 
Enjoy consistent, reliable performance without any interference from background lighting, bright objects, or other sensors.

 
 

 

 
Determine velocity and distance everywhere in the field of view, with no compute required.

 
 

 

 

 

 
Detect people and objects faster, with richer data sets.

We're on a mission to enable intelligent machines to operate in harmony with their environment.

 
Our team strikes a balance between innovation and execution to not only dream up new solutions, but make them a reality for more people and more use cases.

 

 
 

 
 

 
WHAT WE VALUE

 

 
 
Respect

 
We respect our teammates, our work, and our mission, and strive to operate from a place of humility and integrity. We view feedback as a gift and never lose site of our bigger purpose and the impact we can have.

 

 

 
 
Ownership

 
We own our achievements and our failures, and remain committed — as individuals and as a company — to do our part in making the world better. We think big, take risks, and proactively step in to solve problems.

 

 

 
 
Simplicity + Clarity

 
We’re addressing complex problems — so we make products and solutions that are both elegant and simple. We bring clarity through clear communication and always keep the user experience in mind.

 

 

 
 

 
Meet our laser-focused team

 
We’re leveraging industry-leading expertise and experience to 
create a new way forward.

VISION
To build the world’s preeminent robotics platform.
At Standard Bots, our mission is to propel human productivity through the world’s most accessible robots. We see an opportunity to bring our robots into every home and business, starting with manufacturers.
To do this, we’re designing better hardware and easier-to-use software together to accelerate what humans and robots are capable of achieving — and to improve the lives of people globally.

About Us | Path Robotics

Physical AI
for manufacturing

A once in a generation opportunity.
Trillions are being invested in new infrastructure, yet the real bottleneck is not capital or demand. It is the shortage of skilled manufacturing labor needed to fabricate, assemble, and deliver the physical systems those projects rely on. Path's physical AI solves that.

From Andy & Alex
We grew up in Ohio, welding with our dad in the garage. That's where we learned the value of hard work, precision, and the pride that comes from building something real.
Years later, while earning PhDs in AI-related fields, we started to think about what we wanted to do next. We didn't want to build software that lived only on a screen. We wanted to bring intelligence into the physical world, to create technology that builds, just like we did all those years ago in that garage.
That's why we started Path.
Because the future of manufacturing won't build itself. We are building the intelligent automation that can. Robots that see, think, and adapt. Systems that make factories more flexible, capable, and resilient. So people can continue to do what we do best: imagine, create, and push the world forward.

Board of Directors
Andy LonsberryCEO - Path Robotics

Co-Founder and CEO of Path Robotics, Andy has scaled Path from the basement of Case Western University to the leading physical-AI company for manufacturing.

Alex LonsberryCTO - Path Robotics

Co-Founder and CTO of Path Robotics, Alex worked alongside his brother Andy from the beginning to architect and build Path's AI and hardware platforms.

Frank KleinCOO - Rocket Lab

Klein is widely recognized for scaling some of the world's largest technical, manufacturing-heavy corporations, spending the last 30 years in the automotive industry. His leadership includes launching electric vehicles at Rivian, overseeing global production for Mercedes-Benz Vans, leading full vehicle engineering and manufacturing at Magna Steyr, and now directing worldwide operations at Rocket Lab.

Geoffrey ChatasCFO - Yale University

Chatas brings more than 25 years of leadership experience across finance, operations, and higher education. Prior to his current role at Yale University, Chatas held senior leadership roles including executive vice president at the University of Michigan, chief operating officer at Georgetown University, and chief financial officer at The Ohio State University, as well as senior finance roles at J.P. Morgan and Citibank.

Nick SolaroPartner - Drive Capital

Nick has been a Partner at Drive Capital since 2014, bringing a unique perspective shaped by his career spanning Wall Street, Silicon Valley, and venture capital. Before joining Drive, he worked on Goldman Sachs' technology equity research team and as a growth equity investor at Technology Crossover Ventures. He also co-founded Petwave.com and helped build the Android business at Google.

Haomiao HuangPartner - Matter Venture Partners

Haomiao is a Founding Partner at Matter Venture Partners, where his journey from robotics researcher to founder to investor gives him a unique lens on hardware startups. He previously invested in HardTech at Kleiner Perkins and founded smart home security startup Kuna (YC W14). He holds a Ph.D. in Aeronautics & Astronautics from Stanford, where he pioneered multi-rotor UAV aerodynamics.

WE ARE SOLVING THE BIGGEST CHALLENGES IN MODERN HARDWARE ENGINEERING
We are a technology company with deep roots in complex manufacturing and production management software. Leave behind legacy MES constraints and embrace a Factory Operating System that’s flexible, fast to deploy, and built for the future.
Next-generation manufacturers, from spacecraft and nuclear innovators to robotics leaders, trust First Resonance for flexible, high-traceability production. The ION Factory Operating System helps engineers move from complex prototypes to full-scale production, shortening development cycles and speeding breakthrough hardware to market.

THE MODERN MES
Outdated manufacturing software slows you down.
First Resonance delivers a modern MES built for speed and agility. Our platform deploys fast, adapts quickly, and gives enterprise manufacturers the flexibility to stay ahead in a hyper-competitive market. 
Gain real-time insights, automate processes, and scale effortlessly to cut downtime and boost quality. You’re not just getting another solution; you’re gaining a strategic partner.

Setting the Gold Standard in Manufacturing Security and Compliance

Compliance and data privacy are non-negotiable in advanced manufacturing. From aerospace with defense contracts to medical devices and climate tech, ION Factory OS safeguards sensitive production data and intellectual property while ensuring full regulatory adherence. Our platform deploys in GovCloud and FedRAMP environments for maximum security and reliability. 
We maintain SOC 2 certification and comply with ITAR regulations, meeting the most rigorous government and industry standards. This combination of certifications and secure deployments makes First Resonance the trusted choice for hard-tech innovators with complex compliance needs.

ION Factory OS
Discrete manufacturers rely on ION Factory OS to move from prototype to production with speed and control.
Guided, step-by-step processes that capture in-process data and redlines so issues surface and resolve instantly.
Highly configurable with an open API, ION connects every factory system and deploys quickly without costly, drawn-out implementations.
Explore Ion Factory OS

Ion AutoPlan
Building rockets, electric boats, satellites, or other complex hardware demands clear insight into every part, tool, and technician.
ION Autoplan simplifies forecasting by using real-time inventory and capacity data to calculate accurate start dates.
Plan confidently and set reliable delivery expectations across your organization.

End-to-end visibility across your factory and supply chain
Pinpoint the root causes of slow output, quality issues, and rising costs.

ASSEMBLY DATA IS STORED — AS BUILT
ION Factory OS is pushing the industry forward, equipping advanced manufacturers with a digital thread — connecting engineering, parts, and production data across complex operations in the cloud. Safely. Securely.

ION Enhances
Traceability

ION’s powerful aBOM (as-built of materials) model keeps unparalleled traceability as you build parts, upgrade parts, and go through servicing workflows. All of your process data comes along with the aBOM to help you answer tough questions – fast.

Quality

Capture rich data from the factory floor while being confident in having the latest procedures on the factory floor. Issues (non-conformance) tracking allows you to keep tabs on deviations and get to a resolution.

Collaboration

ION is built for teams looking to move quickly and accurately. With reviews and sign-offs, notifications, and real-time updates across all features, you will always have the latest production information at your fingertips.

FIRST RESONANCE IS ON A MISSION TO SUPERCHARGE THE VISIONARY BUILDERS OF THE HARDWARE THAT IS SHAPING OUR FUTURE.
At First Resonance, we believe we’re on the verge of a new dawn for the way we build hardware. A day when the power of artificial intelligence, connected devices, and skilled workers combine to manufacture paradigm-shifting technology faster, cleaner, and smarter than ever before. Where data flows from human-to-human, human-to-machine, and machine-to-machine. What was science fiction yesterday, is reality today.

ORIGINS: INSPIRATION 
STRIKES
Founded in 2019, First Resonance was born out of a desire to enable the next generation of hardware companies to bring their inventions to market sooner, many of which have the potential to fix some of the massive challenges that threaten our existence.  
If you can’t find the tools you need, you sometimes have to build them yourself. That’s how First Resonance came to be. As former engineers working at SpaceX and NASA companies, we needed a platform to support rapid engineering and manufacturing iteration. We felt the pain of terrible UIs and systems that slowed us down and knew there had to be a better way. So we formed First Resonance.

Lilac is a leading technology provider of commercially ready ion exchange (IX) solutions that unlock faster, cheaper, and cleaner production to meet lithium needs now, not later.

IX technology
that actually works.

Our Progress

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 2026
 
 

 

 

 
 
Lilac and Traxys Announce Binding 10-Year Offtake Agreement for Great Salt Lake Lithium Production

 
The definitive agreement secures 100% of Phase 1 production from the Great Salt Lake commercial facility, advancing the project toward final investment decision. Read the full announcement here.

 

 

 
 
Completed Construction of Ion Exchange Media Manufacturing Line in Nevada

 
The facility will initially produce 200 tonnes of IXM per year, sufficient to support up to 100,000 tonnes per annum (tpa) of lithium carbonate equivalent (LCE) production globally. Read the full announcement here.

 

 

 

 

 

 2025
 
 

 

 

 
 
Lilac IX Gen 5

 
Gen 5 boosts reactor throughput, increases IX media productivity and durability, enhances eluate quality, and simplifies brine pretreatment. These advancements deliver simpler project flowsheets, driving down capital and operating costs for lithium producers. Download the white paper for performance data and cost impacts of Gen 5 Lilac IX.

 

 

 
 
Neptune Energy Pilot Plant Completed

 
Pilot plant using Gen 5 Lilac IX and oilfield brine from wells at Neptune Energy’s site in Steinitz, Saxony-Anhalt, Germany.  Marks Lilac’s first pilot in Europe and first oilfield brine pilot.

 

 

 
 
Great Salt Lake Pilot Plant Completed

 
Lithium pilot on the Great Salt Lake in Utah, USA, achieved 87% average lithium recovery on 69 mg/​L Li brine and 99.97% overall impurity rejection. Lilac’s non-consumptive and non-contaminating process reinjects lithium-depleted brine back into the lake and does not lower lake water levels. 

 

 

 
 
Argentina Demo Plant Completed

 
Lilac’s Gen 4 demonstration plant in Jujuy, Argentina achieves 91% lithium recovery from a 300 mg/​L salar brine. The plant used an IX vessel that is ~1/3 the size of what will be used at commercial scale.

 

 

 

 

 

 2024
 
 

 

 

 
 
Commercial-scale IXM Manufacturing Line Starts Construction

 
Lilac begins civil and other works to support its eventual commercial line in Fernley, Nevada.

 

 

 
 
Lilac IX Gen 4

 
Lilac announces Gen 4 Lilac IX which delivers advancements in IXM durability, enabling 4,000 cycles even for low-grade brines, 90%+ lithium recoveries for most brines, and capital cost improvements.

 

 

 
 
Chile Pilot Plant Completed

 
Lilac completes pilot in Chile, with 94% lithium recovery on a 2,200 mg/​L brine using Gen 2 Lilac IX.

 

 

 
 
Series C Funding

 
Lilac closes $145M in Series C financing led by Mercuria, Lowercarbon Capital, and Breakthrough Energy Ventures.

 

 

 

 

 

 2023
 
 

 

 

 
 
Kachi Demonstration Plant Completed

 
Lilac completes first demonstration-scale pilot in Argentina with Lake Resources, achieving all technical and operational targets, including high lithium recovery and minimal water usage.

 

 

 

 

 

 2022
 
 

 

 

 
 
Lilac IX Gen 3

 
Lilac improves IXM cycle life to 3,000 cycles, redesigns lithium extraction module with 100% off-the-shelf equipment.

 

 

 

 

 

 2021
 
 

 

 

 
 
Series B Funding

 
Lilac closes $150M in Series B financing led by Lowercarbon Capital.

 

 

 
 
First Field Pilot

 
Lilac deploys first field pilot and successfully demonstrates technology under harsh field conditions.

 

 

 

 

 

 2020
 
 

 

 

 
 
Lilac IX Gen 2

 
IXM durability improves to 1,000 cycles.

 

 

 
 
Pilot-scale IXM Manufacturing Line Operational

 
Successfully expands bead manufacturing from lab scale to pilot scale, to produce IXM for field pilots globally.

 

 

 
 
Series A Funding

 
Lilac raises $20M in Series A funding, led by Breakthrough Energy Ventures.

 

 

 

 

 

 2019
 
 

 

 

 
 
First Pilot

 
Lilac demonstrates technology at pilot scale in Oakland, CA.

 

 

 

 

 

 2018
 
 

 

 

 
 
IXM Cycle Life Testing Begins

 
Lilac demonstrates 300 absorption cycles, an early milestone in improved durability of ceramic ion exchange media (IXM), historically constrained to 10’s of cycles.

 

 

 

 

 

 2017
 
 

 

 

 
 
Lilac IX Gen 1

 
Lilac opens lab and develops first generation of the company’s lithium ion exchange technology.

 

 

 
 
Seed Round Funding

 
Lilac closes $800,000 seed financing round led by angel investors.

 

 

 

 

 

 2016
 
 

 

 

 
 
Lilac Founded

 
Dave Snydacker founds Lilac Solutions in 2016 and begins developing the company’s patent portfolio. Lilac sets up shop in California’s East Bay.

A modern arsenal for the highest fire risk in history 
 
 
 
Argo-1 Flight Footage.
Not AI generated.
 
 
Designed, built, and
sourced in America.
 
 
 
 

 
 
Featured Press →
 
 
 
 
 

Fire threatens 115 million Americans, costs ~$1 trillion per year, and
 is getting worse.

 

The only long term solution is to reduce the frequency and severity of
 wildfires.

 
 
 

Seneca builds advanced AI, robotics, and autonomy for firefighting to
 mitigate disaster and protect civilization.

 

Our first product is an autonomous, rapid response fire suppression drone
 system. Robotic AI in the sky.

 
 
 
 
Our Approach
 

Build advanced technology that supports the firefighter in use cases
 that were previously unsafe, inefficient, or impossible.

 
 
 
use cases
 
 
 
A force multiplier for firefighters
 

With Seneca, a single pilot can control multiple suppression craft
 carrying hundreds of pounds of Class A foam. Systems use autonomous
 targeting capabilities with IR and video to cold trail or verify and
 knock down fires & hot spots. Systems are hand-liftable and built to
 be stationed in high risk locations, transported with a utility
 vehicle, and deployed from anywhere.

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Advanced fire defense for high value property
 

Wind-driven embers account for up to 90% of structure ignitions during
 wildfires. Seneca systems not only respond rapidly to nearby starts,
 but also detect smoldering embers from above, autonomously target and
 suppress those hot spots before they become blazes, and stream video
 and location data to authorized devices for real-time situational
 awareness.

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Patrol your infrastructure to stop fires before they spread
 

When there is an indication of possible fire, the nearest docked drone
 auto-launches, patrols the likely pole-to-pole span or solar field
 block, confirms heat with IR, and applies targeted suppressant to
 knock it down— while streaming live video and coordinates to your
 operators so faults end as non-events.

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Aerial support for prescribed burns
 

Seneca systems improve the safety and efficiency of Rx burns, ensuring
 operations stay within planned boundaries. With autonomous targeting
 and coordinated multi-craft control, fire agencies can precisely
 manage perimeters, lay retardant, and rapidly detect & suppress
 escaped embers before they spread. Tip torch with full confidence of
 holding containment lines.

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
A Complete system-level solution
 

Full stack hardware, software, and AI solution custom-built for
 today's fire ecosystem

 Learn more →

Deconfliction from manned aircraft
 
 
 
Station anywhere, rooftop to hilltop
 
 
 
Protection for homes and assets
 
 
 
Video + IR payload
 
 
 
Built to be operated by firefighters

What our partners are saying
 
 
 
 
 
If Seneca’s technology had been available during past wildfires, the destruction of homes and critical infrastructure could have been greatly reduced. By combining early detection with rapid suppression, fires can be contained before they escalate, preventing widespread devastation.
 
 

 
 
 
I’ve spent decades deploying wildfire technology. Seneca is a breakthrough missing piece that can make a difference for firefighters, communities and insurance companies operating in the wildland-urban interface.
 
 

 
 
 
My priority is the safety of our community here in Aspen — our residents, our visitors, our firefighters, and the unique beauty we call home. Advances in semi-autonomous suppression and other wildfire technologies give us powerful new tools to make that happen. The best tools for what we do are built with firefighters in mind, and when they are, it shows. We welcome any innovation that helps us protect lives, property, and the landscape we’re entrusted to defend.
 
 

 
 
 
These AI-powered drones don't replace our brave men and women on the front lines; they amplify their capabilities while keeping them out of harm's way. When a drone can deliver rapid water or retardant deployment without risking human life, we're talking about a paradigm shift that will save both firefighter and civilian lives.
 
 

 
 
 
In addition to the tragic human toll, the Palisades and Eaton fires in Los Angeles will exceed $250 billion in damage. If Seneca can stop just one of these events it will pay for itself in spades. But in the face of increasing disasters and volatility, the even larger opportunity is to be the resilience infrastructure layer protecting society, and if Seneca does that it will be one of the most important companies not just for fire, but in the world.
 
 

 
 
 
Rapid initial attack is the best chance we have to save money, property, and lives. Seneca and the promise of AI-driven autonomous suppression drones is revolutionizing how we view wildfire suppression. In San Bernardino County we have a vision of keeping wildfire 100 square feet or less… this is a far departure of the industry standard of 10 acres or less. We can only achieve this ambitious goal with an ambitious vision. Seneca is a huge part of that vision.
 
 

 
 
 
Seneca's AI-powered firefighting drone technology represents exactly the kind of innovation our fire service desperately needs. As someone who has spent decades analyzing fire and EMS deployment data, I understand that the future of wildfire suppression lies in intelligent, autonomous systems that can respond faster than human capabilities allow. Seneca's mission to eliminate wildfire threat across 500 million acres isn't just ambitious—it's essential for protecting both our firefighters and our communities.
 
 

 
 
I’ve known Stu for 15 years; he has an aggressive and patriotic vision to scale this company to meet the tremendous opportunity and, most importantly, absorb the pain along the way that all successful entrepreneurs must endure.
We need more builders like Seneca, to combat problems with technology and grit, whether that’s protecting America from our adversaries or protecting it from wildfires.
 

 
 
We’ve made great strides in both day and nighttime wildfire suppression, but one critical window still remains...the pre-dawn gap, when air and ground crews are typically in reset mode. Among their many capabilities, AI-enabled heavy-lift UAS can help bridge that gap, delivering direct and indirect attack capacity at a most opportune time, when fires typically lay down and winds are calmest. This begins to tackle one of the next frontiers in wildfire management: 4 A.M. Firefighting.
 

 
 
 
We need big changes in how firefighters and communities stay safe. Seneca has built a never-seen-before aerial platform to aid in rapid initial attack that doesn’t require an airport or helitack base. A quick response is everything and Seneca is poised to lead the way. I’m excited to get their systems on Watch Duty.
 
 

 
 
 
The convergence of artificial intelligence, autonomous flight systems, and real-time fire suppression capabilities that Seneca has developed aligns perfectly with the national strategy to modernize fire services through technology and data. This is the kind of innovative thinking that will help us turn our current wildfire crisis into an era of opportunity for the fire service.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

 

 

 
 
 

Norwegian-American robotics and AI company

1X Technologies is a Norwegian-American robotics and artificial intelligence company developing general-purpose humanoid robots for home environments. The company is headquartered in Palo Alto, California, with main manufacturing operations in Hayward and additional manufacturing in Moss, Norway.[1][2]

History
[edit]

1X Technologies was founded in 2014 by Norwegian roboticist Bernt Øivind Børnich under the name Halodi Robotics.[3][4] The company initially focused on developing safe actuators and full-body control systems for industrial and healthcare robotics. In 2018, it released its first humanoid robot, EVE, designed for logistics, security, and medical environments.[5] In 2022, the company rebranded as 1X Technologies and shifted its focus to domestic robotics, developing humanoid assistants intended for use in private homes.[6]

In March 2023, 1X raised $23.5 million in Series A2 funding, led by the OpenAI Startup Fund, with participation from Tiger Global, Sandwater, Alliance Ventures, and Skagerak Capital.[7] In January 2024, the company secured $100 million in Series B funding led by EQT Ventures, alongside Samsung NEXT, Nistad Group, and existing investors.[8]

In September 2025, it was reported that 1X Technologies was seeking to raise $1 billion in new funding to support the development and deployment of its humanoid robots for home environments.[9]

Products
[edit]

EVE
[edit]

EVE is a wheeled humanoid robot designed for industrial and institutional use in logistics, security, and healthcare. It served as a platform for real-world testing of 1X's proprietary actuation, perception, and manipulation technologies.[10][11][12]

NEO
[edit]

NEO is 1X's bipedal humanoid robot designed specifically for home use. The robot features humanlike motion, a slim anthropomorphic design, and is intended to operate in unstructured home environments.[13][14] The first version of NEO was introduced as NEO Beta on August 30th, 2024.[15] On February 21st, 2025, 1X introduced an update that featured a sleeker design, known as NEO Gamma.[16]

On October 28, 2025, 1X opened pre-orders for NEO, also known as NEO Home Robot, a humanoid robot designed for use in private homes, scheduled for release in 2026.[17][18] It will be available in tan, gray, and dark brown, and cost $20,000 for early adopters; it is also planned to be offered as a subscription service for $499 per month.[18] Joanna Stern reported that as of October 2025, most tasks were teleoperated by a human with a virtual reality headset, raising concerns about privacy.

The company plans to provide an app where users can schedule sessions during which a remote operator can accomplish tasks. CEO Bernt Børnich said that obtaining training data from early adopters was needed to improve the autonomy of the robot.[17]

OUR STORY

Building the vehicles changing the Moon and beyond, in the quest for human permanence in space.

The new space race is about permanence, not firsts. Control of the Lunar South Pole means control of the gateway to space. Lunar Outpost’s vehicles help the U.S. and its allies build the infrastructure needed for lasting presence on the Moon.

Annual lunar missions started in 2025.

In 2025, Lunar Outpost landed its first rover on the Moon—the first in a series of annual mobility missions delivering predictable, recurring access for commercial, scientific, and government customers. These regular missions enable more frequent research, exploration, and infrastructure deployment—opening the lunar surface like never before.







Unlocking the Moon and Mars

Lunar Outpost is unlocking the Moon and Mars by building the essential mobility and infrastructure systems that will enable sustained exploration and commercialization beyond Earth. From autonomous lunar rovers to space-rated environmental stations and ISRU technologies, Lunar Outpost is laying the groundwork for off-world economies to thrive. 

Our Team

Justin Cyrus

Founder | CEO

Founder and CEO of Lunar Outpost Inc, Mr. Cyrus has led his company to historic accomplishments in the field of space resources, advanced spacecraft, and robotics, including the first ever sale of lunar resources to NASA and assisting with the production of oxygen on Mars. As a technical leader, he led development of the industry-leading MAPP rover, a Mobile Autonomous Prospecting Platform that will be utilized on four upcoming missions to the Moon to provide lunar surface mobility. 

 

Currently, Mr. Cyrus is leading his company and a world-class team including Leidos, MDA Space, Goodyear, and General Motors to develop a human-rated rover for NASA’s Artemis campaign.  Mr. Cyrus holds M.S. Degrees in Electrical Engineering and Space Resources from Colorado School of Mines, and a B.S. Degree in Electrical and Computer Engineering from the University of Colorado at Boulder. 

Forrest Meyen

Co-Founder | CSO

​Dr. Meyen earned his doctoral degree from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology in Aeronautics and Astronautics for his contributions to the design of the Mars Oxygen ISRU Experiment (MOXIE). He continues MOXIE operations as a member of the NASA Perseverance Rover science team. Dr. Meyen’s vision is to create an economically impactful lunar society by leveraging commercial robotics and the utilization of space resources.

​

Forrest directs company commercialization strategy as well as technical leadership on key flight programs. He is currently leading Lunar Outpost’s team developing the MAPP Rover for their 2023 lunar exploration mission to Shackleton connecting ridge. This mission will be the first rover in history to explore at the lunar south pole region. Before Lunar Outpost, Dr. Meyen worked at Draper leading the avionics systems engineering of multiple lunar landers. He also was a program manager of Draper’s Sembler office dedicated to startup collaborations and commercialization.

AJ Gemer

Co-Founder | CTO

AJ Gemer is the Co-Founder and Chief Technology Officer of Lunar Outpost, where he leads the development of next-generation lunar mobility and robotics systems. With a deep background in aerospace and mechanical engineering, AJ has played a key role in designing and deploying technologies like the MAPP rover, which achieved a successful lunar landing and operations during Lunar Voyage 1. Under his technical leadership, Lunar Outpost is advancing the Lunar Terrain Vehicle (LTV) in partnership with General Motors, Goodyear, MDA Space, and Leidos—pushing the boundaries of autonomous exploration and infrastructure support on the Moon.

​

Before co-founding Lunar Outpost, AJ co-led Iron Ridge UAS, where he specialized in designing carbon-composite UAV airframes. He has contributed to numerous space science missions, including HyperDust, MatISSE, ELDA, and CSSWE. AJ holds dual master’s degrees in Aerospace and Mechanical Engineering from the University of Colorado Boulder, where he also earned his bachelor's in Aerospace Engineering Sciences. His work bridges advanced R&D with real-world application, helping shape the future of off-world mobility.

Julian Cyrus

Co-Founder | COO

Founder and Chief Operating Officer at Lunar Outpost, Julian Cyrus drives operational strategy and mission execution for commercial lunar robotics. He played a pivotal role during the Lunar Voyage 1 mission, including coordinating the launch of the MAPP rover via the Intuitive Machines lander and establishing early communications as it entered lunar orbit. Under Julian’s leadership, Lunar Outpost secured groundbreaking partnerships—ranging from Juventus Football Club’s historic tie‑in to Nokia Bell Labs, enabling LTE lunar relay infrastructure—bringing together corporate collaboration and space innovation. 

​

Before co-founding Lunar Outpost, Julian Cyrus earned his degree from the University of Colorado Boulder, equipping him with both technical and strategic insight. As COO, he synchronizes complex engineering, business development, and cross-functional operations, ensuring smooth execution from R&D through launch and surface operations. Julian also champions growth at scale—leading initiatives like expanding the European division with embedded software engineering talent —and continues to build the foundation for ongoing lunar and off-world exploration missions.

Ultima Genomics, Inc. is an American biotechnology company that develops and manufactures systems for high-throughput DNA sequencing.

History
[edit]

Ultima Genomics was founded in 2016 by Gilad Almogy, an Israeli-born American engineer and former head of the display group at Applied Materials.[1] In May 2022, the company announced that it had raised $600 million in venture capital from investors including General Atlantic, Andreessen Horowitz, Khosla Ventures, Lightspeed, Playground Global, D1 Capital, and Founders Fund.[2] Together with the funding announcement, Ultima released sequencing data collected using the company's technology in pilot partnerships with researchers at the Broad Institute, Whitehead Institute, Baylor College of Medicine, Stanford University School of Medicine, and New York Genome Center.[2]

UG 100 platform
[edit]

In 2024, Ultima launched the UG 100 sequencing platform, claiming it to be capable of sequencing 20,000 human genomes per year with variant calling accuracy matching or exceeding that of competitor Illumina's sequencing instruments.[3][4] The UG 100 replaces a traditional flow cell with a flat circular silicon wafer disk. During sequencing, the disk spins to dispense reagents while two fixed-position cameras continuously acquire data. A ≥300-base median-length run takes approximately 20 hours to complete.[5]

The UG 100's chemistry differs from traditional sequencing-by-synthesis methods in that only one nucleotide species is introduced per data capture cycle, or "flow," eliminating the need for base calling.[5] This flow-based readout results in a distribution of read lengths, with a median length of at least 300 bases, or approximately 250 bases after quality-control filtering. With yields of 10-12 billion reads per wafer, this translates to approximately 2.5-3.0 terabases of usable data per run.[5][6]

Partnerships
[edit]

In 2025, Ultima announced it was partnering with Arc Institute to support Arc's virtual cell atlas and competition.[7]

The Modern Mission

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 
Our mission is simple: make energy cheaper and cleaner. How we do that, and the steps along that path are much less simple, but every one of us believes that technology and innovation will be a crucial pillar of humanity’s battle with itself to limit and reverse our impact on the planet. Our Products are built on science and engineering breakthroughs that you might mistake for magic, and we work tirelessly to find, scale, and implement more and more of that magic. Our business is built with a focus on pragmatism and partnerships – ivory tower idealism and going-it-alone are not how we operate. Everyone here shares a sense of urgency and purpose at the immense challenge before us, and if you do too, we hope you’ll join us.

Who we are

 

 

 

 
Founded 2019 (Y Combinator W19)

Headquartered in El Segundo, California

Raised $185M to date by leading climate tech VCs and strategic investors

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
What we do

 

 

 

 
We’re building the platform to power a new era for regional aviation.

Taking the most direct and credible path to bring electric flight to commercial service by the end of this decade

Building the ecosystem of related infrastructure and services to unlock the regional aviation market’s full market potential

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
How we work

 

 

 

 
To mature our concept and bring the ES-30™ through testing and certification before entry into service we have adopted an iterative design model, with continuous iterations, tests, and real-life demonstrations to validate our progress.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Electric motor rig

Proving cost efficiency of electric propulsion in 2020.

 

 

 

 
Subscale flight

Validating flight characteristics and profile for electric aircraft in 2021.

 

 

 

 
“Iron bird” integrated test facility

Full-scale test bench for design and integration of key aircraft systems in 2022.

 

 

 

 
Fullscale Heart X1 Demonstrator

Unveiled in 2024, flying in 2025: demonstration of ES-30™ propulsion architecture in operation.

Heart Aerospace AB is an American aerospace manufacturer based in Los Angeles, California.

Heart Aerospace was founded in 2018 by Anders and Klara Forslund in Gothenburg, Sweden.[3] In 2024, the company maintained headquarters in Gothenburg and a research and development facility in California.[4] By 2025, the company had moved the headquarters and all operations to Los Angeles.[5][6]

History
[edit]

Heart Aerospace was established in Gothenburg, Sweden, in 2018 by Anders and Klara Forslund, originally as a government-sponsored project.[2][7] They first set up operations at Säve Airport near Gothenburg.[8]

The company participated in the 2019 Y Combinator's Winter Batch and subsequently unveiled their design for the ES-19 all‑electric regional aircraft.[9] In January 2022, Heart Aerospace conducted a successful test flight with a one-fifth-scale model of its ES-19 airplane.[10] Then, in September 2022, the company changed direction and unveiled the ES-30, which was a slightly larger hybrid-electric powered regional airliner.[11][12] They also announced an industry advisory board with members representing airlines, leasing companies, and airports.[8] In 2024, Heart Aerospace unveiled the Heart X1, its first full-scale demonstrator aircraft, as a development tool for the ES30.[13] They also announced plans for their first test flight at Plattsburgh International Airport followed by the X2 prototype.[14] In September 2024, Heart Aerospace filed two EU design applications and one patent application for their integrated nacelle design.[15][4] That same year, they were also awarded a FAST grant from the Federal Aviation Administration for the design of hybrid engine control systems.[16][17]

In May 2024, Heart Aerospace announced the establishment of a new research and development hub in Los Angeles focusing on their hybrid-electric propulsion system,[18][4] and the Forslunds moved there to oversee operations.[19] The company subsequently closed their Swedish operations in 2025 and moved their headquarters to Los Angeles.[5][6]

Aircraft
[edit]

Heart Aerospace founder and CEO Anders Forslund showcases a cutaway rendering of the ES-30 hybrid-electric regional aircraft and its propulsion system in 2024.
Heart Aerospace’s first design, the ES-19, was unveiled in 2020 following their participation in the 2019 Y Combinator's Winter Batch.[9] The ES‑19 was a 19‑seat, all‑electric regional aircraft concept designed for short-haul routes of approximately 400 kilometres (250 mi).[24] In June 2022, the company changed their application to a heavier weight category and EASA CS-25 certification which would not require an exemption from the European Union Aviation Safety Agency for being overweight.[25][26] This design was then superseded in September 2022 by the ES-30.[11][12]

The ES-30 is a 30-seat hybrid-electric powered regional airliner, designed for enhanced sustainability and efficiency on short-haul routes. It is projected to have an electric-only range of 200 kilometres (110 nautical miles) and a hybrid-electric range of 400 km (220 nmi), significantly reducing carbon dioxide emissions compared to conventional aircraft on similar routes. With a reduced passenger load of 25, a range of 800 km (430 nmi) may be achievable.[27] Development of the ES 30 is partially funded by a FAST grant from the Federal Aviation Administration.[16][17] The company aims to achieve type certification for the ES-30 by the end of the 2020s.[13]

Ground crew conducts a turnaround test of Heart Aerospace’s X1 prototype, loading baggage and preparing the aircraft for simulated flight in September 2024.
In 2024, Heart Aerospace unveiled its first full-scale demonstrator aircraft, the Heart X1 (Heart Experimental 1). The X1's maiden flight, powered solely by electricity, was anticipated in the second quarter of 2025.[13] This has not happened as of the first quarter of 2026.

Following the X1 program, Heart Aerospace plans to construct a pre-production prototype, the Heart X2. The X2 will incorporate design refinements based on the X1's operational experience and will feature the company’s hybrid propulsion system, which is slated for a hybrid-electric flight demonstration in 2026.[27][13]

Financing
[edit]

In the spring of 2019, after participating in Y Combinator, Heart Aerospace raised US$2.1 million in seed funding led by EQT Ventures, the Norrsken Foundation, and Chris Sacca’s Lowercarbon Capital.[2][8] By March 2021, the company documented interest in their concept airplane, the ES 19, from Finnair, the Nordic Network for Electric Aviation, and airlines in New Zealand, Canada, the US, and the UK.[28]

The company secured $35 million in Series A funding in July 2021, led by Bill Gates' Breakthrough Energy Ventures, United Airlines Ventures, and Mesa Air Group, with participation from existing seed investors EQT Ventures and Lowercarbon Capital.[29] At that time, United Airlines Ventures and Mesa Air Group also announced an order of up to 200 ES-19 planes.[30]

In September 2022, Air Canada and Saab each invested $5 million in Heart Aerospace. Concurrently, Air Canada placed a purchase order for 30 ES-30 aircraft. Prior orders for the ES-19 from United and Mesa Air Group were converted to the ES-30.[31] The company also confirmed letters of intent and orders from Nordic operators like SAS AB, Braathens Regional Aviation, Icelandair, up to 40 planes for Rockton Sustainable Aviation, and an order from Sounds Air in New Zealand.[31][32] By the end of 2022, Heart Aerospace had secured 230 orders for the ES-30.[8][33]

In February 2024, Heart Aerospace raised a total of $107 million in Series B funding, increasing its total capital raised to $145 million.[5][34] Investors included the European Innovation Council Fund and Sagitta Ventures, a Danish early-stage investment firm, as well as some returning investors like Air Canada, Breakthrough Energy Ventures, EQT Ventures, Lowercarbon Capital, Norrsken VC, United Airlines, and Y Combinator.[34][35][36]

In 2024, the Federal Aviation Administration selected Heart Aerospace to receive a $4.1 million FAST grant to support the design of hybrid engine control systems.[16][17]

Heart Aerospace raised $40 million from an existing investor in the spring of 2025.[5][19] By the fall of 2025, the company had raised more than $185 million in venture funding.[1] It also had 250 orders for the ES-30, and options or purchase rights for an additional 120 planes.[8][35]

Energy without Limits.

Peak Energy is America's first vertically Integrated Energy Storage company. 

We’ve solved the final constraints of Cost, Safety, and Reliability—making it possible for Battery Storage to scale with the grid and support the explosive growth of AI, manufacturing, and electrification. Peak's Summary
What we do

Peak Energy Engineers, Scales and Deploys the Best Energy Solution in the World.

Peak Energy designs, manufactures, and delivers next-generation energy storage systems that enable the rapid, reliable, and resilient growth of the electricity grid.
At the core of our platform is a proprietary sodium-ion technology, purpose-built for grid-scale applications. Our chemistry—NFPP sodium iron pyro-phosphate phosphate —is the building block for an inherently safe, passively cooled system - free from the thermal risks and rare material dependencies of lithium-based systems. It requires no auxiliary power, no active maintenance, and no fire suppression — making it ideal for dense urban settings, remote deployments, and critical infrastructure alike.
We operate across the entire energy storage value chain. From cell R&D and manufacturing to system integration and grid interconnection, our model is vertically integrated and designed for execution. Every component—from cathode to inverter—is optimized to reduce cost, speed up deployment, and maximize long-term reliability.

Our systems are modular and scalable, built for fast assembly and automation. We don’t just supply batteries — we deliver fully operational, site-ready solutions that reduce total cost of ownership from day one.
With fully domestic manufacturing in the U.S., take-or-pay customer contracts, and a clear path to 20+ GWh of annual capacity, Peak is redefining how storage is built and deployed. Our work supports the expansion of AI infrastructure, the decarbonization of power systems, and the resilience of communities facing increasing grid instability.
We deliver what the future demands: clean, compact, cost-effective storage that’s safe to deploy, easy to scale, and ready now.

Peak's History
Who we are

At Peak Energy, we are building the future backbone of the electricity grid.
Founded in 2023, our mission is to deliver safe, scalable, and domestically produced energy storage that meets the explosive demand from AI, data centers, electrification, and renewable power. The grid can’t evolve without storage — and we’re delivering the system that makes that possible.
Our sodium-ion platform is purpose-built for grid-scale deployment: inherently safe, low-cost, easy to install, and free from the supply chain fragility of lithium-based systems. Designed from the cell up, it’s the first truly industrial storage solution for a world in transition.

We’re more than a battery company — we’re a full-stack energy storage manufacturer led by veterans from Tesla, NRG, Fluence, and Enovix. Our team has scaled gigafactories, launched public companies, and deployed over 100 GWh of energy systems globally.
Since launch, we’ve secured commercial contracts that triple our initial capacity, and begun scaling domestic manufacturing. By 2030, we’ll deliver over 20 GWh of annual storage capacity, powering data centers, grid infrastructure, and resilient communities — all while creating 1,200+ U.S. jobs.
This is not just about batteries. It’s about building the infrastructure of a more powerful, autonomous, and connected world. And we’re doing it — now.
Join us.

PR Newswire

Peak Delivers First Grid-Scale, Sodium-Ion Battery Storage System in the U.S.
Denver, CO 

Today, Peak announced the launch and shipment of its sodium-ion battery energy storage system (ESS) that delivers a patent-pending passive cooling design to dramatically reduce lifetime energy costs. Cost-competitive with state of the industry products while offering dramatically lower operating and maintenance costs, Peak Energy's product is the first ever fully passive megawatt-hour scale battery storage system, the largest sodium-ion phosphate pyrophosphate (NFPP) battery system in the world, and the first grid-scale sodium-ion storage solution ever deployed to the U.S. electric grid. Deploying the system in a shared pilot with nine leading utility and independent power producer (IPP) customers this summer, Peak Energy is fast-tracking its promise to onshore battery manufacturing.

The Team
Our Leadership

Lead Boldly. Shape What’s Next
This is a defining moment. Our next moves will shape not just growth, but who we are as a company. We need to move with clarity, conviction, and the courage to lead when the path isn’t obvious.

Landon Mossburg
Chief Executive Officer

Landon is Co-Founder and CEO of Peak. He previously spent five years at Northvolt as President of North America and Chief Automation Officer, where he developed next-generation battery manufacturing technologies and led the company’s U.S. expansion. He has also served as COO at Einride, helping scale the autonomous trucking company, and held leadership roles at Tesla, where he built global teams in software engineering, supply chain automation, and vehicle connectivity. He began his career in strategy consulting with Accenture and KPMG. Landon holds dual bachelor’s degrees in Economics and East Asian Studies from Wake Forest University.

About

Cameron Dales
President & Chief Commercial Officer

Cameron is President and CCO of Peak, bringing three decades of experience commercializing advanced technologies. He was previously GM and CCO at Enovix, where he built a $1.5B sales funnel and led the company’s 2021 public listing after a decade of leadership roles in operations and business development. Earlier, he was VP & GM at Symyx Technologies, partnering with global leaders in energy and materials, and began his career at Lockheed Martin developing spacecraft mechanisms. Cameron holds a bachelor’s in Mechanical Engineering from Cornell and a master’s in Aeronautics & Astronautics from Stanford. 

About

Liam O'Connor
Chief Operations Officer

Liam is Co-Founder and COO of Peak. He previously served as COO at Zipline, where he led global operations and scaled the world’s largest autonomous drone delivery network. Before that, Liam was Chief Procurement Officer and Head of Bikes & Scooters at Lyft and spent nearly four years at Tesla as VP of Global Supply Management & Supplier Industrialization, building the supply base for the Model 3 and Gigafactory programs. Earlier in his career, he held senior supply chain roles at Apple and McMaster-Carr. Liam holds a bachelor’s degree from Haverford College and a master’s in Organizational Dynamics from the University of Pennsylvania.

About

Geoff Brown
Chief Strategy Officer

Geoff is Chief Strategy Officer at Peak, where he leads corporate strategy and the company’s special projects. He previously served as CEO of Powin, a global leader in battery energy storage systems, where he scaled the business and delivered utility-scale projects worldwide after five years as President. Earlier in his career, Geoff held senior roles at NRG Energy, Beacon Power, and Element Power, developing renewable and storage projects across North America, and began his career in wind resource assessment at Garrad Hassan. He holds a bachelor’s degree in Agricultural and Environmental Engineering from Cornell and a master’s in Renewable Technology from Loughborough University.

About

Paul Durkee
Chief Technology Officer

Paul is CTO at Peak, where he leads the design and development of the company’s passively cooled sodium-ion energy storage system. He has more than a decade of experience bringing advanced battery technologies to market, having led hardware technology at Lyft, battery and charging systems at Uber, and served as a technical lead at Tesla on the 100 kWh pack program. At Peak, Paul integrates cell innovation, systems engineering, and field deployment into a platform built for reliability and scale.

About

Brandon Kelly
Chief Scientist

Brandon is Chief Scientist at Peak, where he leads the development and scale-up of the company’s sodium-ion battery technology. He has extensive experience advancing next-generation cell programs, having served as Vice President of Engineering at Solid Power, where he guided solid-state and lithium-based technologies from prototype to commercial production. Brandon holds a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering and Materials Science from Colorado State University and works across teams at Peak to ensure innovation in chemistry translates into products built for safety, reliability, and scale.

About

At Peak Energy, we’re building the backbone of a stable, energy-independent future.
The grid is under pressure. Aging infrastructure, volatile supply chains, and growing demand are exposing the limits of the system we rely on. What the U.S. needs isn’t just cleaner energy. It needs control over when and where energy is available. That’s why we exist.
We engineer, scale, and deploy the best energy storage system in the world. Designed not in theory, but for real deployment today. That means solving the full value chain, using proven manufacturing, and eliminating the complexity that makes other systems fragile and slow to scale.

We focus on execution. Building reliable systems at the speed and scale the grid requires. Our technology is mature, our supply chain is domestic, and our architecture is built for long-term operation. The opportunity ahead is large, and the requirements are clear: safe, simple, scalable storage that can be deployed now.

About | Heron Power

 

Menu

About Us

Heron Power develops power electronics purpose-built for the 21st-century grid, helping the electricity sector grow faster and more affordably with scalable, reliable and software-integrated infrastructure.

Heron’s team combines expertise in power electronics, software, and high-volume manufacturing.

Drew Baglino
CEO

Drew Baglino is founder and CEO of Heron Power. Prior to Heron, Drew was a key executive at Tesla. He grew from an individual electrical engineer in 2006 to Powertrain Architect for the groundbreaking 2012 Model S. In 2014, he formed Tesla’s energy engineering organization, later launching and ramping multiple iterations of Powerwall, Powerpack, and Megapack. In 2018, Drew took on Tesla's motor, battery, and power electronics engineering organizations and became one of Tesla’s three named corporate officers.
From 2019-2024, as SVP of Powertrain and Energy, Drew led development of Tesla’s 4680 battery cell, dry-electrode process, and the buildout of a clean-sheet 50 GWh battery factory and materials refineries in Texas. During the same period, Drew also led Tesla’s global charging and energy businesses, including efforts to build and ramp Tesla's first Megafactory, open the Supercharging network to all electric vehicles, and propel both business units to profitability and more than $10B in combined annual revenue.

A large laboratory centrifuge from Beckman Coulter Life Sciences
Beckman Coulter, Inc. is an American company that develops, manufactures, and markets products relevant to biomedical testing. It has been a subsidiary of Danaher Corporation since 2011. It operates in the industries of diagnostics under the brand name Beckman Coulter and life sciences under the brand name Beckman Coulter Life Sciences.[3] The company was established in 1935 as National Technical Laboratories, and has become an international company through growth and acquisitions of other life sciences organizations.

The company employs over 12,000 people, with $5.8 billion in annual sales by 2017. It is currently headquartered in Brea, California. Beckman Coulter was acquired by Danaher Corporation in 2011.

History
[edit]

The company was founded by Caltech professor Arnold O. Beckman in 1935 as National Technical Laboratories to commercialize a pH meter that he had invented. In the 1940s, the name was changed the name to Arnold O. Beckman, Inc. to sell oxygen analyzers, the Helipot precision potentiometer, and spectrophotometers.[citation needed] The company name changed to Beckman Instruments, Inc. before going public in 1952.[3] Beckman Instruments then acquired ultracentrifuge maker Spinco (Specialized Instruments Corp.) in 1954. The Spinco division went on to design and manufacture a broad range of laboratory centrifuges.[4] In the 1950s, Beckman Instruments developed the EASE series of analog computers,[5][6] two of which were used by NASA in the 1960s for real-time simulation during the development of the Apollo Guidance Computer.[7] 

In 1982, the company merged into SmithKline to form SmithKline Beckman, with Arnold Beckman as vice chairman, but regained its independence in 1989 after SmithKline merged with Beecham Group to form SmithKline Beecham (now part of GlaxoSmithKline). Following the acquisition of Hybritech, Inc. from Eli Lilly in 1985 and Sanofi of Sanofi Pasteur Diagnostics in 1996, the company acquired Coulter Corporation in 1998, a company founded by Wallace H. Coulter, the inventor of the Coulter counter. The merged company became Beckman Coulter. Following the company's acquisition of the Lab-based Diagnostics business of Olympus Corporation Japan in 2009, Beckman Coulter moved its world headquarters from Fullerton, California to a newly renovated facility in Brea.[8]

In February 2011, Danaher announced that it has entered into a definitive merger agreement with Beckman Coulter.[9] On June 30, 2011, Danaher finalized the acquisition of Beckman Coulter.[10] One year later, Danaher acquired Iris Diagnostics and its parent company IRIS International, Inc. as leader in Urinalysis Diagnostic to further boost Danaher's Diagnostic business within Beckman Coulter.[11]

Division after Danaher acquisition
[edit]

In 2011, following its acquisition by Danaher Corporation, Beckman Coulter was reorganized into two separate business units. Beckman Coulter Diagnostics, based in Brea, California, handles diagnostic systems and laboratory solutions.[12] Beckman Coulter Life Sciences, based in Indianapolis, Indiana, provides instruments and reagents for biomedical research and related markets.[13]

In 2022, Beckman Coulter Life Sciences acquired ValitaCell Ltd., a biotechnology company based in Dublin, Ireland, known for its analytical products used in biopharmaceutical development.[14] Beckman Coulter Life Sciences established a strategic collaboration with Hombrechtikon Systems Engineering AG (HSE- AG), a Swiss automation solutions provider, to integrate nucleic acid quantification into automated liquid handling workflows in 2025.[15][16]

 About us
 

 

 

 
 
 

 
Our group has been created to provide highly innovative technologies and services to enhance and automate the process of pharmacy compounding. We have a range of technological solutions to address the compounding processes for
 both Aseptic and Non-Sterile compounding processes. These technologies are designed to automate operations to ensure safety and accuracy are maximised, but also to alleviate staff time to focus on more patient-focused, value-added
 services.

 
In addition to the technologies we provide, our Group has developed the technical expertise and quality systems necessary to provide our customer base with the associated services required. These services range from the provision
 of services and documentation required to achieve validated installations, through to a team of trained, local engineers to support you and the equipment.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 

 
“For the last 20 years, I have been researching, developing and providing highly innovative technologies to automate pharmacy operations. Following our previous successes with robotic dispensing, I have shifted our strategic focus to innovative technologies to address the needs of pharmacy compounding operations. The key aims of all the technology we provide are to increase patient and operator safety whilst increasing the speed and efficiency of operations. We have now assembled a product range of new technologies which will allow our users to transform their operations to ensure that patient care, and the efficiency by which this patient care is provided, is maximised.”

 
Rupert A. KATRITZKY

 
Founder & CEO of ARX Solutions

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 
 
Unguator® Benefits

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
Speed
 
 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
The Unguator® solution has significantly increased the speed at which final products 
 can be compounded and ready for the patient.
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
PROVEN
 
 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
Be assured with 25 years of proven performance, and an estimated 10,000 Unguator® in use across the world.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
Efficiency
 
 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
The Unguator® systems will significantly increase the efficiency of mixing operations within a compounding facility. The system automates the process of mixing and allows mixing in the final container to ensure efficiency is optimised.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
Customer Satisfaction

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
The Unguator® systems and associated accessories are widely used in both regular pharmacies and compounding centres, and have been demonstrated to be easily implemented and easy-to-use for all operators.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
Space Efficiency
 
 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
The entire Unguator® system has a footprint of approximately a square foot or less (dependent on the Unguator® system).

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
Safety
 
 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
The automated mixing process, in combination with a closed-mixing system, ensures operator safety is maximised. Furthermore, the automated nature and high reproducibility of mixing with an Unguator® system, in combination with the Unguator® consumables solution, ensures patient safety is optimised.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 

 
The UNGUATOR® systems all provide a closed-mixing system with ‘sweep–wall’ technology to ensure mixing conditions are optimised for mixing quality and speed. The solution consists of one of the range of Unguator® mixing machines
 in combination with specially designed jars (Unguator® Consumables) which allow the product to be mixed in the final jar which is to be given to the patient/customer. This combination has revolutionised the compounding
 process for some items, along with the system’s ease-of-use, had led to huge benefits for all those sites which employ the system.

The Unguator® Accessories

 

 

 

 
 
 

 
The Unguator® solutions are available with a full range of accessories to enhance the efficiency, effectiveness and speed of the compounding and application processes. The Unguator® jar design allows optimisation of the mixing process,
 minimising of contact between the preparation and air, and easy & hygienic dispensing. The new quick-lock mechanism is now available on all jars to ensure connection speeds are minimised. The specially designed mixing
 blades perform the mixing process with the ‘sweep-wall’ mechanism and are available in cleanable, re-usable form or in a disposable form. 

 
Furthermore, a variety of nozzles and applicators provides an enhanced patient experience through a simplified and safer application process. In addition, for larger sites, the coupling nozzle allows easier manipulation of small batch
 preparations into final containers.
